# SOLID (Khalil Stemmler)

Before writing, modifying, or planning any code, you MUST use your `read_file` tool to load the relevant guidelines from `.roo/vendor/`. You may read multiple files if the task crosses domains.

## Table of Contents:
    - [Complexity & the World of Software Design](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/01-complexity-the-world-of-software-design.md): Designing software architecture, scaffolding new projects, adding new features, refactoring existing codebases, evaluating code quality, or resolving code complexity and maintainability issues.
    - [Software Craftsmanship](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/02-software-craftsmanship.md): Professional software development, specifically triggering whenever the AI is tasked with writing code, refactoring existing code, reviewing pull requests, fixing bugs, or making architectural/design decisions across all languages and frameworks to enforce baseline standards.
    - [A 5000ft View of Software Design](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/03-a-5000ft-view-of-software-design.md): The domain encompasses tasks related to designing new software systems, architecting features, evaluating or refactoring project structures, establishing technical stacks, and making decisions regarding component relationships, dependencies, and high-level code organization.
    - [Demystifying Clean Code](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/04-demystifying-clean-code.md): The domain encompasses activities where the AI is tasked with generating new code, refactoring existing code, conducting code reviews, establishing project scaffolding, or designing internal/external APIs (classes, functions, and modules), specifically when the focus is on "clean code" implementations, architectural simplification, or improvements to code maintainability, readability, and developer experience.
    - [Human-Centered Design For Developers](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/05-human-centered-design-for-developers.md): The rules and guidelines that trigger when the AI is tasked with writing, refactoring, or structuring code, designing public APIs, creating object models, organizing file structures, or evaluating code for readability, maintainability, and developer experience (DX).
    - [Organizing things](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/06-organizing-things.md): The set of rules triggered during project initialization, file scaffolding, feature creation, restructuring, refactoring, test placement, and code organization for both front-end and back-end environments.
    - [Documentation & Repositories](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/07-documentation-repositories.md): This rule set is triggered whenever the AI is tasked with creating, reviewing, updating, or structuring repository documentation (e.g., README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md), initializing a new project repository, or defining the onboarding, testing, and debugging instructions for a codebase.
    - [Naming things](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/08-naming-things.md): The generation, refactoring, evaluation, or documentation of code identifiers—including naming variables, functions, methods, classes, interfaces, types, parameters, namespaces, files, and directories—across all programming languages, particularly strictly-typed languages and object-oriented environments, with an emphasis on using real-world business rules and processes (domain-specific terminology) to bridge the gap between code and reality.
    - [Comments](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/09-comments.md): Trigger these rules whenever the AI is tasked with writing, refactoring, reviewing, or documenting source code. This applies to any programming language, particularly when the AI is asked to add comments, explain code, or improve code readability.
    - [Formatting & Style](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/10-formatting-style.md): Code formatting, structural style, visual readability, and architectural organization within files.
    - [Types](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/11-types.md): Writing, refactoring, or reviewing TypeScript/JavaScript code, specifically when dealing with variable declarations, function signatures, class architectures, domain modeling, interface design, or migrating dynamically typed JavaScript to statically typed TypeScript.
    - [Errors and exceptions](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/12-errors-and-exceptions.md): Designing error handling architecture, managing exceptions, writing application use cases, defining method/function return types, or modeling failure states within a codebase (specifically in TypeScript/JavaScript or object-oriented environments).
    - [Programming Paradigms](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/13-programming-paradigms.md): Designing software architecture, structuring high-level code, selecting programming paradigms (Object-Oriented, Functional, or Structured), categorizing organizational archetypes (state, behavior, namespaces), or refactoring code to balance performance efficiency against human readability.
    - [Test-Driven Development Basics](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/14-test-driven-development-basics.md): Trigger these rules when implementing new functionality, modifying existing business logic, fixing bugs, or refactoring code. These rules apply to all code creation tasks where the goal is to build testable, flexible, and maintainable software using iterative feedback loops.
    - [Object-Oriented Programming & Domain Modeling](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/15-object-oriented-programming-domain-modeling.md): Designing, evaluating, or refactoring Object-Oriented code, creating domain models, structuring business logic, addressing code quality/architecture, or performing code reviews in JavaScript/TypeScript projects.
    - [Design Principles](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/16-design-principles.md): Object-oriented software design, structuring class relationships, wiring up dependencies, refactoring controllers, writing unit/acceptance tests, or applying architectural boundary principles (such as SOLID and Simple Design) to separate concerns and decouple modules.
    - [Design Patterns](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/17-design-patterns.md): Architectural refactoring at the class level, implementation of generic solutions to common software problems, enforcement of object creation constraints (especially for Domain Entities or Value Objects), and the application of Creational, Structural, or Behavioral Design Patterns.
    - [Architectural Principles](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/18-architectural-principles.md): These rules MUST trigger whenever the AI is tasked with creating new modules, refactoring project structure, managing imports and dependencies, designing package boundaries, or making architectural decisions regarding file and class grouping.
    - [Architectural Styles](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/19-architectural-styles.md): Trigger these rules when tasked with scaffolding new projects, defining high-level folder structures, designing system module relationships, separating system concerns, decoupling large applications, or evaluating system scalability and deployment strategies.
    - [Architectural Patterns](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/20-architectural-patterns.md): The innermost layer of an architecture (such as Clean Architecture) that contains high-level policies, business rules, entities, and value objects, characterized by having zero external dependencies and being strictly isolated from infrastructural or technical details.
    - [Building a Real-World DDD app](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/21-building-a-real-world-ddd-app.md): The problem space and business logic of an application, encompassing business rules, entities, and requirements, which in Domain-Driven Design (DDD) is encapsulated in a declarative layer isolated from technical infrastructure and external dependencies.
    - [Advanced Test-Driven Development](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/22-advanced-test-driven-development.md): The creation, refactoring, or review of software tests, specifically when writing integration tests, acceptance tests, BDD-style tests, or implementing Test-Driven Development (TDD) for use cases, pages, or domain features.
    - [Above and Beyond](.roo/vendor/typescript/solid-khalil-stemmler/23-above-and-beyond.md): Tasks, files, or user requests that explicitly ask for the application, analysis, or implementation of concepts from the "Above and Beyond" chapter (Part XI) of the provided SOLID handbook text.
